findings.evidence: Existing API at lines 74, 85, and 92 defines `groupLike`, `groupLike_coact_apply`, and `groupLike_coact`; the new declaration at lines 217-218 is exactly `groupLike ... (g * h)`.
findings.fix: Remove `groupLikeTensor_*` and use `Comodule.groupLike (M := M ⊗[R] N) (g * h)` with the existing `groupLike_coact_apply`/`groupLike_coact` lemmas.
findings.issue: `Comodule.groupLikeTensor` and its coaction lemmas are a parallel API for `Comodule.groupLike` specialized to `M ⊗[R] N` and `g * h`.

findings.evidence: Existing constructor `Hom.ofGroupLike` is at lines 99-109; the new body at line 265 is exactly `ofGroupLike ... (g * h) (TensorProduct.map f k)`.
findings.fix: Remove `tensorGroupLike_*` and use `Hom.ofGroupLike (g * h) (TensorProduct.map f k)` plus existing `ofGroupLike_*` lemmas and `TensorProduct.map` lemmas.

findings.evidence: Existing `trivial` and `Hom.ofTrivial` are at lines 341 and 361; the new definitions at lines 431-432 and 468-478 are just those APIs instantiated with `M ⊗[R] N` and `TensorProduct.map f k`.
findings.fix: Remove these declarations and use `Comodule.trivial (M := M ⊗[R] N)` and `Hom.ofTrivial (TensorProduct.map f k)` directly.
findings.issue: `Comodule.trivialTensor` and `Hom.tensorTrivial` duplicate the existing trivial comodule and trivial morphism APIs on the tensor-product module.

summary: The added tensor-product comodule API is definitional duplication of existing group-like and trivial comodule constructors and morphism constructors.
